introduce.label: Introduce
introduce.title: What is a CSV to QBO Converter
introduce.description: A CSV to QBO converter transforms bank transaction exports into the QBO (OFX) format that QuickBooks Online and Desktop accept for bank feed imports. Our free tool runs entirely in your browser, so you can import Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, and other bank CSVs without desktop software.

items.title: Upload Bank CSV
items.description: Drag a bank or credit card .csv export into the browser or paste CSV text directly. This CSV to QBO workflow reads your transaction rows and prepares them for QuickBooks import.

items.title: Smart Column Mapping
items.description: Auto-detect date, description, and amount columns from your bank export. Adjust mappings for separate debit and credit columns when needed.

items.title: Preview Transactions
items.description: Review normalized dates, descriptions, and signed amounts before generating your QBO file. Catch mapping errors before importing into QuickBooks.

items.title: Download QBO File
items.description: Download a .qbo file with FITID transaction IDs and proper OFX structure. Import it into QuickBooks via Banking > Upload transactions.

how_to_use.label: How it works
how_to_use.title: How to convert CSV to QBO online
how_to_use.description: This CSV to QBO converter is built for bank statement imports. Upload your CSV, map columns, preview transactions, and download a QuickBooks-ready file in four steps.

items.title: Upload or paste your bank CSV
items.description: Export transactions from your bank as CSV and upload the file, or paste the text directly. The CSV to QBO tool auto-detects common column names like Date, Description, and Amount.

items.title: Map your columns
items.description: Confirm which columns contain the transaction date, description, and amount. Switch to debit/credit mode if your bank uses separate columns.

items.title: Preview transactions
items.description: Review the normalized transaction list before generating the QBO file. Verify dates, amounts, and descriptions look correct.

items.title: Download and import to QuickBooks
items.description: Click Download QBO, then in QuickBooks Online go to Banking > Upload transactions and select your .qbo file.

items.title: Duplicate-safe imports

csv_qbo_guide.title: Why convert CSV to QBO for QuickBooks
csv_qbo_guide.description:
QuickBooks bank feed imports work best with QBO (OFX) files because they include structured metadata — transaction IDs, account types, and date ranges — that plain CSV lacks.

While QuickBooks Online can import CSV directly, the process requires manual column mapping every time and often fails on date or amount formats. A CSV to QBO converter handles the OFX structure automatically so your import succeeds on the first try.

items.title: Structured import
items.description: QBO files include FITID transaction IDs and proper OFX headers that QuickBooks reads natively.

items.title: Any bank CSV
items.description: Map columns from any bank export — single amount or separate debit/credit columns.

items.title: Private by design
items.description: All CSV to QBO conversion runs locally in your browser — your financial data never leaves your device.

faq.title: CSV to QBO FAQ
faq.description: Common questions about CSV to QBO conversion, QuickBooks import, and how to use this free online tool.

items.question: How do I convert a CSV file to QBO online?
items.answer: Upload your bank CSV or paste the text, map your date, description, and amount columns, preview the transactions, then click Download QBO. Import the file in QuickBooks via Banking > Upload transactions.

items.question: What is a QBO file?
items.answer: A QBO file is a bank statement in OFX (Open Financial Exchange) format with Intuit-specific headers. QuickBooks uses it to import transactions into the bank feed with duplicate detection via FITID codes.

items.question: Which banks are supported?
items.answer: Any bank that exports transactions as CSV with date, description, and amount columns. This includes Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Citi, Capital One, and hundreds of others worldwide.

items.question: Is my financial data uploaded?
items.answer: No. All CSV to QBO conversion happens entirely in your browser. Your bank CSV never leaves your device.

items.question: What if my bank uses separate Debit and Credit columns?
items.answer: Switch to Separate debit & credit mode in the column mapping step. Select your Debit and Credit columns and the converter will merge them into signed amounts.

items.question: How do I import the QBO file into QuickBooks Online?
items.answer: In QuickBooks Online, go to Banking > Transactions > Upload transactions. Select your .qbo file, choose the bank account, and review the imported transactions.

items.question: Is CSV to QBO conversion free?
items.answer: Yes. CSV to QBO conversion on CSV Viewer is completely free with no signup, no usage caps, and no hidden fees.
